title: Problem 1.1 (Conlon, Fox, and Sudakov)
statement_text: Show that for any $q \geq 2$, there exists $c_q$ such that $r_3(H; q) \leq 2^{2^{c_q\sqrt{m}}}$ for any 3-uniform hypergraph $H$ with $m$ edges and no isolated vertices.
context_text: Conlon, Fox and Sudakov proved that any $k$-uniform hypergraph $H$ with $m$ edges satisfies $r_k(H; q) \leq \mathrm{tw}_k(c\sqrt{m\log m})$ for $k=3$, and asked whether the logarithmic factor could be removed. This problem was posed in their survey on graph Ramsey theory.
paper_contribution: Resolves Problem 1.1 in full: Theorem 1.2 proves $r_k(H; q) \leq \mathrm{tw}_k(C_{k,q}\sqrt{m})$ for all $k \geq 3$ and any fixed number of colors $q$, with a unified proof covering all uniformities.
paper_title: Ramsey numbers of hypergraphs of a given size

title: Informal Problem: extending the $\sqrt{m}$ Ramsey bound to more than two colors for graphs
statement_text: It would be interesting to extend the result $r_2(G; 2) \leq 2^{c\sqrt{m}}$ (for any graph $G$ with $m$ edges and no isolated vertices) to more than two colors.
context_text: Sudakov [22] resolved Erdős's conjecture by showing $r_2(G; 2) \leq 2^{c\sqrt{m}}$, but the argument works only for two colors. The paper notes this gap immediately after mentioning the resolution of the conjecture.
notes: Stated as a brief remark in the introduction without a labelled environment.
paper_title: Ramsey numbers of hypergraphs of a given size
